title: Pentacalia popayanensis
taxonomy: Pentacalia popayanensis is classified within the family Asteraceae, specifically within the tribe Senecioneae. This classification places it among other daisy-like plants. The genus Pentacalia is known to comprise around 200 species, primarily found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. Within the Asteraceae family, Pentacalia popayanensis is one of the species that exhibit a unique combination of characteristics. The classification of Pentacalia popayanensis provides insight into its evolutionary relationships with other plants.

intro: Pentacalia popayanensis is a plant species within the family Asteraceae, native to the Andean region of South America. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to the treatment of various diseases such as arthritis and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Pentacalia popayanensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
